_comment: Copy this file to config.json and fill in your lab's values. Then run: python -m utils.secrets set --termino  (and similar for other secrets).

mail_provider._comment_type: Pick one: 'uni-graz-ews' (Outlook/Exchange Web Services), or 'yahoo-smtp' (Yahoo SMTP with app-password).

sheet_provider._comment_type: Pick one: 'unicloud' (Nextcloud WebDAV at cloud.uni-graz.at) or 'google' (legacy Google Sheets download).

calendar_provider._comment_type: Pick one: 'none' (skip calendar entirely), 'unicloud-caldav' (Nextcloud calendar at cloud.uni-graz.at), or 'uni-graz-ews' (Outlook calendar).
calendar_provider._comment_verified: 'unicloud-caldav' is the production-verified path (live on the LXC daily run). 'uni-graz-ews' is supported as an alternative.
calendar_provider._comment_share: Optional: add 'share_with': ['user1', 'user2'] to auto-share the calendar with VLs.

auto_vpn._comment_enabled: When true, the script auto-spawns openconnect-sso to authenticate (SAML/Keycloak) and openconnect to bring up the VPN tunnel before the daily workflow, tearing it down after. The right backend is picked per OS by utils.vpn_provider. Linux: needs the OS-level setup from docs/SERVER_VPN_SETUP.md (sudoers, xvfb, openconnect-sso). Windows: needs Administrator PowerShell + openconnect-gui + openconnect-sso (uv tool) + DOM-selectors config.toml; see docs/WINDOWS_SETUP.md. macOS: no-op for now, use Cisco Secure Client manually.
auto_vpn._comment_windows: Windows-only fields. Verified 2026-06-01 on openconnect.exe v9.12 (openconnect-gui-1.6.2-win64). The script needs Administrator PowerShell to create the Wintun adapter. stop_cisco_during_run and stop_mullvad_during_run temporarily stop those services to avoid routing conflicts; they are restarted on exit.
